key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share

key_assumptions.source: Cloud AI mix shift; normalize post-capex ramp

open_questions: Confirm live price and diluted share count against latest filing.
Promote AI inflection or third-party views into base case only after human OK.
thesis: Alphabet Inc. owns Google (Search, YouTube, Android, Chrome) and Google Cloud. **[Fact]** Advertisers and enterprises pay for ad auctions, cloud compute, and subscriptions; consumers use free products that drive query volume and data. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$402.8B** (+15%); Q1 2026 revenue **$109.9B** (+22%). **[Fact]** Reportable segments are **Google Services**, **Google Cloud**, and **Other Bets** (Waymo, etc.), plus **Alphabet-level activities** (centralized AI R&D). **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated operating cash flow **$125.3B**, capital spending **$52.5B**, owner free cash flow **~$72.8B**; management guides **$180-190B** capital spending in 2026. **[Inference]** Owner economics depend on Cloud return on invested capital versus the capital spending step-up and whether Search monetization holds as interfaces shift to AI.
why_market_wrong: There is **no clear dated mispricing signal** (predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** At **~$386**, the stock prices Cloud AI optionality and Search resilience together. **[Inference]** A single consolidated free cash flow growth rate hides **divergent segment economics**: Services throws off most cash today while Cloud and Alphabet-level spend absorb the 2026 capital guide. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math sums to **~$348 per share** base economic value versus **~$386** price, implying the market embeds higher Cloud multiples or faster capex normalization than the bounded proof schedule.
